summary: Asian PP homo fell sharply USD 90-130/tonne; buyers cautious
Sabic S-Oil July settlement USD 1,020/tonne CIF (down USD 20)
Hanwha Korea cheapest at VND 30,000/kg for early July
paras: According to ICIS pricing published on the last trading day of the previous week, 26/6, PP resin in Vietnam continued to fall sharply, with PP yarn/injection down USD 70/tonne in the low segment and USD 130/tonne in the high segment, to USD 1,040-1,130/tonne/CFR. More broadly across the region, PP homo prices in Southeast Asia and China fell sharply by USD 90-130/tonne, while PP block copo and random copo dropped sharply by USD 90-120/tonne.

The market came under stronger downward pressure as numerous competitive offers emerged from South Korea. Low-priced Korean cargoes weakened buying sentiment and made it difficult for other supply sources to close transactions. Buyers continue to stay on the sidelines, concerned that prices may fall further in the near term.

Asian PP prices fell as declining crude oil dragged down naphtha and propylene feedstock prices, while demand stayed weak. Buyers remain out of the market on expectations that a deal between the US and Iran, together with the subsequent reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, will push crude oil prices even lower. Notably, some Korean suppliers have actively offered cargoes to Vietnam at low prices, creating very strong competitive pressure. China-origin goods are less attractive as their prices are kept high. However, with many PP production complexes in China restarting in June and export volumes declining, the country's inventories are expected to become more surplus, and the flow of goods into Southeast Asia in the coming period is forecast to increase.

Back to the Vietnam market, today, 29/6, Sabic's distributor announced the settlement price for PP yarn/injection produced at the Korean plant (S-Oil) for Vietnamese buyers, with cargoes delivered at the end of July 2026. Accordingly, this month's PP yarn/injection settlement price is USD 1,020/tonne/CIF, USD 20/tonne lower than the bid price the distributor announced at the start of last week. No settlement price has yet been recorded for PP copo.

A large distributor offered several China-origin PP homo products to the Vietnam market, although its offer prices were quite high relative to the general level. The distributor offered China's Jinneng PP yarn/injection at USD 1,095/tonne/CIF; Sinopec PP yarn T30S at USD 1,120/tonne/CIF; PP yarn 1102K at a high of USD 1,205/tonne/CIF; CNPC (PetroChina) PP yarn L5E89 at USD 1,085/tonne/CIF – down USD 20/tonne from last week; and CNPC PP copo K8003 at USD 1,130/tonne/CIF.

Sources said the above distributor's offer prices are unlikely to result in transactions. In fact, late last week a distributor offered Jinneng PP yarn/injection to Vietnam at USD 1,025/tonne/CIF, while S-Oil Korea PP was just quoted by Sabic at a settlement price of USD 1,020/tonne/CIF…

Domestically, today a leading NSRP distributor offered NSRP's PP 114G at VND 31,000/kg (VAT and delivery included) in the north, down VND 500/kg from the offer at the end of last week – equal to late last week's settlement price.

Notably, BSR's PP T3034 was offered by a leading distributor at VND 31,400/kg (VAT included) ex-factory warehouse in Quang Ngai – down by as much as VND 1,000/kg from late last week. Sold to the south this offer adds VND 600/kg for VND 32,000/kg (VAT included), and offered to the north adds VND 800/kg for VND 32,200/kg (VAT included)…

Sources said the NSRP and BSR plants are still running at maximum capacity, and PP inventories remain quite high as export volumes in the second half of June have slowed compared with the first half of June.

The cheapest remains PP of South Korea's Hanwha origin. A trader offered PP HJ500 at just VND 30,000/kg (VAT included) for cargoes arriving in early July, while Hanwha's PP copo BJ300, BJ350 and BJ550 are holding flat at VND 33,000-34,000/kg (VAT included)…

summary: US offers: LLDPE USD 850-880; HDPE USD 930-970/tonne CIF
Sabic settlements fell sharply; buyers close small orders
paras: According to ICIS pricing published on the last trading day of the previous week, 26/6, LDPE offer prices in Vietnam fell USD 100/tonne in the low segment and USD 190/tonne in the high segment over the past week, down to USD 1,200-1,230/tonne/CFR; LLDPE prices fell USD 20/tonne in the low segment and dropped sharply by USD 100/tonne in the high segment, down to USD 1,000-1,030/tonne/CFR; HDPE Film fell USD 70-100/tonne to USD 1,030-1,050/tonne/CFR; HDPE injection fell USD 95/tonne to USD 1,055-1,155/tonne/CFR; HDPE blow fell USD 50/tonne to USD 1,100-1,200/tonne/CFR…

PE import negotiation activity in Asia declined sharply over the past week, under pressure from a wave of low-priced US cargoes coupled with weak buying demand. In the major Southeast Asian markets, regional and Middle Eastern suppliers are continuously cutting prices. The impact of US PE offer prices is most evident in Vietnam, which recorded the lowest offer prices in the region, although most of these transactions and offer levels could not be verified with suppliers or traders.

Today, 29/6, on the first trading day of the new week, several sources reported that the market saw LLDPE offers (near/off grade) of US origin at USD 850-880/tonne/CIF, and HDPE Film and blow offers at USD 930-970/tonne/CIF. Most of these offered cargoes have unclear grades and unspecified manufacturers; according to suppliers, they are traded goods that have been repackaged.

LDPE and LLDPE from Saudi Arabia's Sabic had their settlement prices announced late last week (CIF cargoes from Singapore in July), and distributors are now focusing on negotiating to close orders; the HDPE settlement price has not yet been announced. Although Sabic's PE settlement prices this month fell very sharply to fairly competitive levels, buyers signing import contracts remain cautious and are mostly closing small-volume orders (Sabic's LDPE Film settled at USD 1,300/tonne/CIF, LLDPE film at USD 1,030/tonne/CIF, and mLLDPE C6 at USD 1,250/tonne/CIF).

Domestically, after very deep declines last week, PE offer prices are being adjusted more cautiously at the start of this week. Many sellers are trying to hold prices to observe further market developments, amid tense armed conflict between the US and Iran at the end of last week. Even so, downward price pressure remains strong.

Currently, Sabic's LLDPE Film 218WJ offers are common around VND 33,000/kg (VAT included), while China-origin LLDPE Film has dropped to VND 32,000/kg (VAT included). For HDPE, the lowest offer is Qamar's HDPE Film F1 at VND 36,000/kg (VAT included), while US- and China-origin HDPE Film/blow is at VND 34,000-35,000/kg (VAT included). LDPE prices across origins range from VND 41,000-44,000/kg (VAT included)…

summary: Asian PVC fell to USD 690-720/tonne CFR; imports USD 670-700 CIF
China demand recovery slow post-holiday; producers cut export prices
Domestic coal SG-5 VND 20,500/kg; oil-based AGC Vina VND 20,900/kg
paras: According to ICIS pricing, the Asian PVC market came under downward price pressure over the past week amid abundant regional supply, while downstream demand remained weak.

After the Dragon Boat (Tết Đoan Ngọ) holiday, China's domestic consumption recovered slowly, forcing the country's producers to cut export prices to stimulate demand and strengthen competitiveness in Southeast Asia. In India, import demand was also subdued due to the rainy season and uncertainties surrounding import-duty exemption policy. PVC prices in Southeast Asia fell to USD 690-720/tonne/CFR.

Today, 29/6, transaction prices for imported PVC (oil-based) into Vietnam are commonly in the range of USD 670-700/tonne/CIF. Buyers are maintaining fairly good inventory levels and only purchasing additional volumes when necessary, amid continued declines in crude oil and naphtha prices, which heighten expectations that PVC prices still have room to fall.

In domestic trading, coal-based PVC SG-5 remains the lowest at VND 20,500/kg (VAT included) in the north, while oil-based PVC PM66R from domestic producer AGC Vina has the lowest price at VND 20,900/kg (VAT included). The previous week's price floor remains temporarily unchanged. However, in the higher-priced segment, some sellers continue to cut prices to compete.

For example, PVC SE950/SE1000 originating from Shintech (USA) has been recorded with a large distributor offering at VND 21,500/kg (VAT included), significantly below the general price level of the previous week…
